words.meaning_en: Open to more than one interpretation; not clear
words.example_sentence: His ambiguous reply left everyone confused.
words.fill_blank: The politician gave an ______ answer to avoid controversy.

words.meaning_en: Well-meaning and kindly; generous
words.example_sentence: The benevolent king shared his wealth with the poor.
words.fill_blank: The ______ donor funded the school's new library.

words.meaning_en: An event causing great damage or distress; a disaster
words.example_sentence: The flood was a terrible calamity for the village.
words.fill_blank: The earthquake was a massive ______ that destroyed hundreds of homes.

words.meaning_en: Having or showing care and effort in work
words.example_sentence: She was a diligent student who never missed a class.
words.fill_blank: A ______ worker always completes tasks on time.

words.meaning_en: Fluent and persuasive in speaking or writing
words.example_sentence: The eloquent speaker moved the audience to tears.
words.fill_blank: Her ______ speech won the debate competition.

words.meaning_en: Careful with money and avoiding waste
words.example_sentence: Despite being rich, he led a frugal life.
words.fill_blank: Being ______ helped her save enough money for college.

words.meaning_en: The quality of being thankful; appreciation for kindness received
words.example_sentence: She expressed her gratitude with a heartfelt letter.
words.fill_blank: He felt deep ______ towards his teacher for all the support.

words.meaning_en: Arrogantly superior and disdainful
words.example_sentence: His haughty attitude made him unpopular among classmates.
words.fill_blank: The ______ prince refused to listen to anyone's advice.

words.meaning_en: Certain to happen; unable to be avoided
words.example_sentence: Change is inevitable in life.
words.fill_blank: It was ______ that the old building would eventually collapse.

words.meaning_en: Feeling or expressing great happiness and triumph
words.example_sentence: The team was jubilant after winning the championship.
words.fill_blank: The crowd was ______ when the home team scored the winning goal.

words.meaning_en: Eager and enthusiastic; very interested
words.example_sentence: She was keen to learn new languages.
words.fill_blank: He was ______ on joining the science club.

words.meaning_en: To express grief or sorrow; to mourn
words.example_sentence: She lamented the loss of her old school friends.
words.fill_blank: The villagers began to ______ the passing of their beloved elder.

words.meaning_en: Showing great attention to detail; very careful
words.example_sentence: The scientist was meticulous in recording her observations.
words.fill_blank: A ______ approach to revision helps avoid silly mistakes.

words.meaning_en: Famous for something bad; having a bad reputation
words.example_sentence: The town was notorious for its floods every monsoon.
words.fill_blank: He was ______ for always arriving late to class.

words.meaning_en: No longer in use; out of date
words.example_sentence: Cassette tapes have become obsolete in the digital age.
words.fill_blank: Fax machines are now considered ______ in most offices.

words.meaning_en: To continue doing something despite difficulty
words.example_sentence: She persevered with her studies despite many challenges.
words.fill_blank: You must ______ through hardship to achieve your goals.

words.meaning_en: An angry argument or disagreement
words.example_sentence: The two friends had a quarrel over a silly misunderstanding.
words.fill_blank: They had a ______ but made up by the end of the day.

words.meaning_en: Unwilling and hesitant
words.example_sentence: He was reluctant to admit his mistake in front of everyone.
words.fill_blank: She was ______ to leave her hometown for a new city.

words.meaning_en: Calm, peaceful, and untroubled
words.example_sentence: The serene lake reflected the blue sky perfectly.
words.fill_blank: She felt ______ sitting by the quiet river at sunset.

words.meaning_en: Of little value or importance
words.example_sentence: Don't waste your energy on trivial matters.
words.fill_blank: They argued over a ______ issue that didn't really matter.
